## [00:00 - 03:03]

Alright guys, so welcome to this part of the training where we're going to discuss verticals and niches. Now when you start working in or when you start building your agency I should say, you really want to start focusing on specializing in a certain niche or vertical. And the reason for that is, well there's a lot of reasons. So firstly, if you can get results for a specific kind of client then what that means is you'll be able to leverage that specific result or testimonial or case study to acquire even more of those clients. So you're leveraging existing work that you've done and results to bring in more business into your agency. And this can be hard to do if you're jumping all over the place. So for example, if you're on a phone call with a real estate agent and he's asking you what kind of results you've been able to achieve for realtors but all your case studies are in e-commerce, you're not able to leverage that as much versus if you get on a call with an e-commerce lead and they ask you the same question and you say to them, look we were able to get four times ROI for an e-commerce client within two months. That's powerful stuff, right? That's the kind of stuff that's going to make your entire sales process a lot easier going forward and build that momentum in your business. So that's one of the reasons we want to pick a niche. The second reason we want to hone in on a specific vertical is that you start to, I guess, focus your team's skills in a way that is replicatable and also build in processes that can be somewhat automated. So I'll give you an example of what I mean by that. In Eight Loop, we work with coaches and we have worked with so many online coaches at this stage that when we onboard a client, my team knows exactly what to do. It's just almost like second nature. They know the types of funnels, they know what works, they know what doesn't work. There's not many learning curves involved. If anything, we're trying to put the client on a learning curve. And this wouldn't be the case if I allowed my agency to jump all over the place. So if Eight Loop was taking on lead generation clients, we would essentially be jumping all over the place and my team would always be on a learning curve. And learning curves cost money and they cost a lot of time. So if you can hone in on a specific vertical and build a team around that, you're going to get into a flow in your agency where a client comes in and it's just streamlined and you can get really good results for that client because your team just knows that vertical.

## [18:16 - 21:22]

So let's say that you've decided to work with e-commerce. Now there's a lot of different e-commerce out there, right? There's brand new e-commerce, there's existing e-commerce, there's hell, there's even influencers trying to do e-commerce, right? And each one of those have specific pain points and wants. So very different pain points. So what you need to do is you really need to map out those problems that they're facing and then connect that to your solution in your copy.
